sections.label: Section 1225
sections.original: οἰκουρόν , οἴμοι , τῷ μολόντι δεσπότῃ
sections.translation: a watchman waiting ( ah me !) for my master ’ s coming home — yes , my master , for I must bear the yoke of slavery . The commander of the fleet and the overthrower of Ilium little knows what deeds shall be brought to evil accomplishment by the hateful hound , whose tongue licked his hand , who stretched forth her ears in gladness ,

sections.label: Section 1230
sections.original: ἄτης λαθραίου , † τεύξεται [*] κακῇ τύχῃ .
sections.translation: like treacherous Ate . Such boldness has she , a woman to slay a man . What odious monster shall I fitly call her ? An Amphisbaena [*] ? Or a Scylla , tenanting the rocks , a pest to mariners ,

sections.label: Section 1235
sections.original: θύουσαν Ἅιδου μητέρ ἄσπονδόν τ Ἄρη [*]
sections.translation: a raging , devil ’ s mother , breathing relentless war against her husband ? And how the all-daring woman raised a shout of triumph , as when the battle turns , the while she feigned to joy at his safe return ! And yet , it is all one , whether or not I am believed . What does it matter ?

sections.label: Section 1240
sections.original: τὸ μέλλον ἥξει . καὶ σύ μ ἐν [*] τάχει παρὼν
sections.translation: What is to come , will come . And soon you , yourself present here , shall with great pity pronounce me all too true a prophetess .

sections.label: Section 1242
sections.original: τὴν μὲν Θυέστου δαῖτα παιδείων [*] κρεῶν
sections.translation: Thyestes ’ banquet on his children ’ s flesh I understood , and I tremble . Terror possesses me as I hear the truth , nothing fashioned out of falsehood to resemble truth .

sections.label: Section 1245
sections.original: τὰ δ ἄλλ ἀκούσας ἐκ δρόμου πεσὼν τρέχω .
sections.translation: But as for the rest I heard I am thrown off the track .

sections.label: Section 1246
sections.original: Ἀγαμέμνονός σέ φημ ἐπόψεσθαι μόρον .
sections.translation: I say you shall look upon Agamemnon dead .

sections.label: Section 1247
sections.original: εὔφημον , ὦ τάλαινα , κοίμησον στόμα .
sections.translation: To words propitious , miserable girl , lull your speech .

sections.label: Section 1248
sections.original: ἀλλ οὔτι παιὼν τῷδ ἐπιστατεῖ λόγῳ .
sections.translation: Over what I tell no healing god presides .

sections.label: Section 1249
sections.original: οὔκ , εἴπερ ἔσται [*] γ : ἀλλὰ μὴ γένοιτό πως .
sections.translation: No , if it is to be ; but may it not be so !

sections.label: Section 1250
sections.original: σὺ μὲν κατεύχει , τοῖς δ ἀποκτείνειν μέλει .
sections.translation: You do but pray ; their business is to slay .

sections.label: Section 1251
sections.original: τίνος πρὸς ἀνδρὸς τοῦτ ἄχος [*] πορσύνεται ;
sections.translation: What man is he that contrived this wickedness ?

sections.label: Section 1252
sections.original: ἦ κάρτα τἄρ [*] ἂν παρεκόπης [*] χρησμῶν ἐμῶν .
sections.translation: Surely you must have missed the meaning of my prophecies .

sections.label: Section 1253
sections.original: τοὺς γὰρ τελοῦντας [*] οὐ ξυνῆκα μηχανήν .
sections.translation: I do not understand the scheme of him who is to do the deed .

sections.label: Section 1254
sections.original: καὶ μὴν ἄγαν γ Ἕλλην ἐπίσταμαι φάτιν .
sections.translation: And yet all too well I understand the Greek language .

sections.label: Section 1255
sections.original: καὶ γὰρ τὰ πυθόκραντα : δυσμαθῆ [*] δ ὅμως .
sections.translation: So too do the Pythian oracles ; yet they are hard to understand .

sections.label: Section 1256
sections.original: παπαῖ , οἷον τὸ πῦρ : ἐπέρχεται δέ μοι .
sections.translation: Oh , oh ! What fire ! It comes upon me ! Woe , woe ! Lycean Apollo ! Ah me , ah me ! This two-footed lioness , who mates with a wolf in the absence of the noble lion ,

sections.label: Section 1260
sections.original: κτενεῖ με τὴν τάλαιναν : ὡς δὲ φάρμακον
sections.translation: will slay me , miserable as I am . Brewing as it were a drug , she vows that with her wrath she will mix requital for me too , while she whets her sword against her husband , to take murderous vengeance for bringing me here . Why then do I bear these mockeries of myself ,

sections.label: Section 1265
sections.original: καὶ σκῆπτρα καὶ μαντεῖα περὶ δέρῃ στέφη ;
sections.translation: this wand , these prophetic chaplets on my neck ? Breaking her wand , she throws it and the other insignia of her prophetic office upon the ground , and tramples them underfoot You at least I will destroy before I die myself . To destruction with you ! And fallen there , thus do I repay you . Enrich with doom some other in my place . Look , Apollo himself is stripping me

sections.label: Section 1270
sections.original: χρηστηρίαν ἐσθῆτ , ἐποπτεύσας δέ με
sections.translation: of my prophetic garb — he that saw me mocked to bitter scorn , even in this bravery , by friends turned foes , with one accord , in vain — but , like some vagrant mountebank , called beggar , wretch , starveling , I bore it all .

sections.label: Section 1275
sections.original: καὶ νῦν ὁ μάντις μάντιν ἐκπράξας ἐμὲ
sections.translation: And now the prophet , having undone me , his prophetess , has brought me to this lethal pass . Instead of my father ’ s altar a block awaits me , where I am to be butchered in a hot and bloody sacrifice . Yet , we shall not die unavenged by the gods ;

sections.label: Section 1280
sections.original: ἥξει γὰρ ἡμῶν ἄλλος αὖ τιμάορος ,
sections.translation: for there shall come in turn another , our avenger , a scion of the race , to slay his mother and exact requital for his sire ; an exile , a wanderer , a stranger from this land , he shall return to put the coping-stone upon these unspeakable iniquities of his house . For the gods have sworn a mighty oath

sections.label: Section 1285
sections.original: πράξειν [*] νιν [*] ὑπτίασμα κειμένου πατρός .
sections.translation: that his slain father ’ s outstretched corpse shall bring him home . Why then thus raise my voice in pitiful lament ? Since first I saw the city of Ilium fare what it has fared , while her captors , by the gods ’ sentence , are coming to such an end ,

sections.label: Section 1290
sections.original: ἰοῦσα πράξω : [*] τλήσομαι τὸ κατθανεῖν .
sections.translation: I will go in and meet my fate . I will dare to die . This door I greet as the gates of Death . And I pray that , dealt a mortal stroke , without a struggle , my life-blood ebbing away in easy death , I may close these eyes .

sections.label: Section 1295
sections.original: ὦ πολλὰ μὲν τάλαινα , πολλὰ δ αὖ [*] σοφὴ
sections.translation: O woman , pitiful exceedingly and exceeding wise , long has been your speech . But if , in truth , you have knowledge of your own death , how can you step with calm courage to the altar like an ox , driven by the god ?

sections.label: Section 1299
sections.original: οὐκ ἔστ ἄλυξις , οὔ , ξένοι , χρόνον [*] πλέω .
sections.translation: There is no escape ; no , my friends , there is none any more . [*]

sections.label: Section 1300
sections.original: ὁ δ ὕστατός γε τοῦ χρόνου πρεσβεύεται .
sections.translation: Yet he that is last has the advantage in respect of time .

sections.label: Section 1301
sections.original: ἥκει τόδ ἦμαρ : σμικρὰ κερδανῶ φυγῇ .
sections.translation: The day has come ; flight would profit me but little .

sections.label: Section 1302
sections.original: ἀλλ ἴσθι τλήμων οὖσ ἀπ εὐτόλμου φρενός .
sections.translation: Well , be assured , you brave suffering with a courageous spirit .

sections.label: Section 1303
sections.original: οὐδεὶς ἀκούει ταῦτα τῶν εὐδαιμόνων . [*]
sections.translation: None who is happy is commended thus .

sections.label: Section 1304
sections.original: ἀλλ εὐκλεῶς τοι κατθανεῖν χάρις βροτῷ .
sections.translation: Yet surely to die nobly is a blessing for mortals .

sections.label: Section 1305
sections.original: ἰὼ πάτερ σοῦ τῶν [*] τε γενναίων τέκνων .
sections.translation: Alas for you , my father and for your noble children ! She starts back in horror

sections.label: Section 1306
sections.original: τί δ ἐστὶ χρῆμα ; τίς σ ἀποστρέφει φόβος ;
sections.translation: What ails you ? What terror turns you back ?

sections.label: Section 1307
sections.original: φεῦ φεῦ .
sections.translation: Alas , alas !

sections.label: Section 1308
sections.original: τί τοῦτ ἔφευξας ; εἴ τι μὴ φρενῶν στύγος .
sections.translation: Why do you cry alas ? Unless perhaps there is some horror in your soul .

sections.label: Section 1309
sections.original: φόνον [*] δόμοι πνέουσιν αἱματοσταγῆ .
sections.translation: This house stinks of blood-dripping slaughter .

sections.label: Section 1310
sections.original: καὶ πῶς ; τόδ ὄζει θυμάτων ἐφεστίων .
sections.translation: And what of that ? It is just the savor of victims at the hearth .

sections.label: Section 1311
sections.original: ὅμοιος ἀτμὸς ὥσπερ ἐκ τάφου πρέπει .
sections.translation: It is like a breath from a charnel-house .

sections.label: Section 1312
sections.original: οὐ Σύριον ἀγλάισμα δώμασιν λέγεις .
sections.translation: You are not speaking of proud Syrian incense for the house .

sections.label: Section 1313
sections.original: ἀλλ εἶμι κἀν δόμοισι κωκύσουσ ἐμὴν
sections.translation: Nay , I will go to bewail also within the palace my own and Agamemnon ’ s fate . Enough of life !

sections.label: Section 1315
sections.original: ἰὼ ξένοι .
sections.translation: Alas , my friends , not with vain terror do I shrink , as a bird that fears a bush . After I am dead , bear witness for me of this — when for me , a woman , another woman shall be slain , and for an ill-wedded man another man shall fall .

sections.label: Section 1320
sections.original: ἐπιξενοῦμαι ταῦτα δ ὡς θανουμένη .
sections.translation: I claim this favor from you now that my hour is come .

sections.label: Section 1321
sections.original: ὦ τλῆμον , οἰκτείρω σε θεσφάτου μόρου .
sections.translation: Poor woman , I pity you for your death foretold .

sections.label: Section 1322
sections.original: ἅπαξ ἔτ εἰπεῖν ῥῆσιν οὐ [*] θρῆνον θέλω
sections.translation: Yet once more I would like to speak , but not a dirge . I pray to the sun , in presence of his latest light , that my enemies [*] may at the same time pay to my avengers a bloody penalty for

sections.label: Section 1325
sections.original: ἐχθροῖς φονεῦσι τοῖς ἐμοῖς τίνειν ὁμοῦ ,†
sections.translation: slaughtering a slave , an easy prey . Alas for human fortune ! When prosperous , a mere shadow can overturn it [*] ; if misfortune strikes , the dash of a wet sponge blots out the drawing .

sections.label: Section 1330
sections.original: καὶ ταῦτ ἐκείνων μᾶλλον οἰκτείρω πολύ .
sections.translation: And this last I deem far more pitiable than that . Enters the palace

sections.label: Section 1331
sections.original: τὸ μὲν εὖ πράσσειν ἀκόρεστον ἔφυ
sections.translation: It is the nature of all human kind to be unsatisfied with prosperity . From stately halls none bars it with warning voice that utters the words Enter no more .

sections.label: Section 1335
sections.original: καὶ τῷδε πόλιν μὲν ἑλεῖν ἔδοσαν
sections.translation: So the Blessed Ones have granted to our prince to capture Priam ’ s town ; and , divinely-honored , he returns to his home . Yet if he now must pay the penalty for the blood shed by others before him , and by dying for the dead

sections.label: Section 1340
sections.original: ποινὰς θανάτων ἐπικράνῃ , [*]
sections.translation: he is to bring to pass retribution of other deaths [*] , what mortal man , on hearing this , can boast that he was born with scatheless destiny ? A shriek is heard from within

sections.label: Section 1343
sections.original: ὤμοι , πέπληγμαι καιρίαν πληγὴν ἔσω .
sections.translation: Alas ! I am struck deep with a mortal blow !

sections.label: Section 1344
sections.original: σῖγα : τίς πληγὴν ἀυτεῖ καιρίως οὐτασμένος ;
sections.translation: Silence ! Who is this that cries out , wounded by a mortal blow ?

sections.label: Section 1345
sections.original: ὤμοι μάλ αὖθις , δευτέραν πεπληγμένος .
sections.translation: And once again , alas ! I am struck by a second blow .

sections.label: Section 1346
sections.original: 1 . τοὔργον εἰργάσθαι δοκεῖ μοι βασιλέως οἰμώγματι . [*]
sections.translation: The deed is done , it seems — to judge by the groans of the king . But come , let us take counsel together if there is perhaps some safe plan of action .

sections.label: Section 1348
sections.original: 2 . ἐγὼ μὲν ὑμῖν τὴν ἐμὴν γνώμην λέγω ,
sections.translation: — I tell you my advice : summon the townsfolk to bring rescue here to the palace .

sections.label: Section 1350
sections.original: 3 . ἐμοὶ δ ὅπως τάχιστά γ ἐμπεσεῖν δοκεῖ
sections.translation:
— To my thinking we must burst in and charge them with the deed while the sword is still dripping in their hands .
— I , too , am for taking part in some such plan , and vote for action of some sort . It is no time to keep on delaying .
— It is plain . Their opening act

sections.label: Section 1355
sections.original: τυραννίδος σημεῖα πράσσοντες πόλει .—
sections.translation:
marks a plan to set up a tyranny in the State .
— Yes , because we are wasting time , while they , trampling underfoot that famous name , Delay , allow their hands no slumber .
— I know not what plan I could hit on to propose . It is the doer ’ s part likewise to do the planning .

sections.label: Section 1360
sections.original: 8 . κἀγὼ τοιοῦτός εἰμ , ἐπεὶ δυσμηχανῶ
sections.translation:
— I too am of this mind , for I know no way to bring the dead back to life by mere words .
— What ! To prolong our lives shall we thus submit to the rule of those defilers of the house ?
— No , it is not to be endured . No , death would be better ,

sections.label: Section 1365
sections.original: πεπαιτέρα γὰρ μοῖρα τῆς τυραννίδος .—
sections.translation:
for that would be a milder lot than tyranny .
— And shall we , upon the evidence of mere groans , divine that our lord is dead ?
— We should be sure of the facts before we indulge our wrath . For surmise differs from assurance .

sections.label: Section 1370
sections.original: ταύτην ἐπαινεῖν πάντοθεν πληθύνομαι ,
sections.translation: — I am supported on all sides to approve this course — that we get clear assurance how it stands with Atreus ’ son .

sections.label: Section 1372
sections.original: πολλῶν πάροιθεν καιρίως εἰρημένων
sections.translation: Much have I said before to serve my need and I shall feel no shame to contradict it now . For how else could one , devising hate against a hated foe
